dsl_rule.translation_notes: Double Bottom pattern requires identifying two distinct lows with a rise between them. Operationalized as: (1) price closing above prior low indicating bounce from second bottom, (2) volume surge above 20-period historical average marking end of downward plunge per source text, (3) RSI below 50 confirming oversold conditions typical of bottoms. Exit when price breaks below prior low. Pattern-specific spacing/separation checks (distance between bottoms, adequacy of rise) cannot be directly encoded in single-bar indicators and would require multi-bar lookback logic, so approximated through volume and momentum confirmation.

source_rule_text: Double Bottom: A retest of a prior low after a downward price trend, with two bottoms separated sufficiently and a rise between them adequate to delineate two minor lows, with small price variation between the two bottoms
